Entreprise Générale du Cobalt (EGC) — state-backed enterprise intended to monopolize artisanal cobalt purchasing (established 2019). RMI (Responsible Minerals Initiative) — industry-led supply chain due diligence program used by electronics and automotive manufacturers.

Ministry of Mines — oversees minería policy, licensing, and the 2018 Mining Code (Code Minier), which increased royalty rates and introduced strategic substance classification for cobalt. CTCPM (Cellule Technique de Coordination et de Planification Minière) — the technical coordination unit for minería sector planning. ITSCI (ITRI Tin Supply Chain Initiative) — a traceability program for tin, tantalum, and tungsten (3T) minerals from the DRC.

Parlement de la République démocratique du Congo — bicameral legislature consisting of the Assemblée nationale (500 members) and the Sénat (108 members). The DRC is a semi-presidential republic but governance capacity is limited outside major urban centers. EU Battery Regulation (2023/1542) — effective 2027, requires carbon footprint declarations, recycled content minimums, and supply chain due diligence for batteries containing cobalt. OECD Due Diligence Guidance for Responsible Supply Chains of Minerals from Conflict-Affected and High-Risk Areas — the international standard for mineral supply chain responsibility.

CG.s: Oil-dependent economía ($15.7B PIB) with 34.74% combined oil and gas rents — one of Sub-Saharan Africa's highest. TotalEnergies and Eni are major operators. Governance challenges with long-ruling president (Sassou Nguesso since 1997). Significant tropical forest cover.

sectors.w: Congo-Brazzaville's oil production is in long-term decline from mature offshore fields. As extraction costs rise and reserves deplete, the interés superpuesto between international operators and government revenue se debilita — diversification becomes necessary.
bp: Redirect oil revenues to economic diversification (forestry, agriculture, services) through transparent budget allocation. Cuando non-oil sectors generate more employment and revenue, el interés superpuesto en continued extraction from depleting fields breaks.
Implementar EITI-standard per-block revenue and emisiones disclosure. Congo joined EITI in 2004 — extending to ambiental data makes W computable per production unit.
No se puede romper by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report ambiental violations at oil facilities to the Ministry of Environment
Document revenue discrepancies — report to EITI Congo multi-stakeholder group
Report concerns to international operator compliance channels (TotalEnergies, Eni)
TotalEnergies, Eni: Implementar Paris-aligned transition plans for Congo operations
SNPC: Desarrollar economic diversification strategy using petroleum revenues
Adoptar per-block emisiones reporting standards
Ministry of Hydrocarbons: Fortalecer ambiental conditions in production sharing contracts
EITI Congo: Expand reporting to include ambiental impact data
Central Bank (BEAC): Integrate resource revenue volatility risk into macroprudential supervision
Abogar a través de CEMAC for regional ambiental standards in oil and gas extraction
Support EITI enhanced reporting with ambiental metrics
Presionar por Central African Forest Initiative (CAFI) enforcement on forest protection as alternative revenue source
Leverage UNFCCC mechanisms for fossil fuel transition support
i: Ministère de l'Environnement, du Développement Durable et du Bassin du Congo — the environment ministry. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company. EITI Congo — the Republic of Congo has been an EITI member since 2004. Note: Whistleblower protections are minimal under current governance.

TotalEnergies — the largest oil operator in Congo-Brazzaville, operating the Moho Nord deepwater project and other fields. Eni — operates significant offshore concessions. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company holding state equity in all production sharing contracts.

Ministère des Hydrocarbures — oversees oil and gas policy and licensing. BEAC (Banque des États de l'Afrique Centrale) — the central bank of the CEMAC monetary zone (shared with Cameroon, CAR, Chad, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon). EITI Congo — publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues.

Note: Congo-Brazzaville under President Sassou Nguesso is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée nationale (151 members) and Sénat (72 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International engagement channels: CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale), African Union, CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) — a partnership between Central African countries and donors to protect tropical forests.

CI.s: Largest economía in francophone West Africa ($87.1B PIB). World's largest cocoa producer (~45% of global supply). Agriculture is 15.9% of PIB with cocoa, coffee, and palm oil as main cash crops. Growing oil production (oil=0.7%). Rapid economic growth (~7% annual average).

sectors.w: Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa monoculture has driven 80%+ of original forest loss. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) will require deforestación-free supply chains — when per-farm deforestación data is linked to cocoa transactions, W se vuelve computable.
bp: EU Deforestation Regulation compliance forces traceability to farm level. Cuando cocoa buyers must verify zero-deforestación, the interés superpuesto between traders and smallholders in clearing forest for new plantations breaks — agroforestry becomes the viable model.

Mandatory per-farm ambiental data (tree cover, soil carbon, water use) linked to cocoa lot traceability. The Cocoa and Forests Initiative already creates a framework — extending to mandatory data makes W computable from cocoa prices.

Parcialmente rompible. Cocoa monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity compared to native forest. However, cocoa agroforestry systems can maintain significant tree cover and biodiversity — Côte d'Ivoire's National REDD+ Strategy includes agroforestry targets.
a: Report illegal deforestación for cocoa expansion to SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) or OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves)
Document child labor in cocoa production — report to CNS (Comité National de Surveillance des actions de lutte contre le travail des enfants)
Report pesticide misuse — notify ANADER (Agence Nationale d'Appui au Développement Rural)
Cocoa traders (Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam): Implementar full farm-level traceability ahead of EUDR deadline
Invertir en cocoa agroforestry systems that maintain tree cover while improving yields
Adoptar living income reference price commitments for cocoa farmers
CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao): Hacer cumplir traceability requirements and link farmgate prices to ambiental compliance
MINEDD (Ministry of Environment): Fortalecer enforcement against deforestación in classified forests
ANADER: Scale agroforestry extension programs to all cocoa-growing regions
Contacte a su Député in the Assemblée nationale para exigir enforcement of the Forest Code and protection of classified forests
Support EU Deforestation Regulation implementation with adequate transition support for smallholder farmers
Abogar a través de the Cocoa and Forests Initiative for binding deforestación targets
Presionar por living income benchmarks in international cocoa pricing through ICCO
i: SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) — the state forestry company responsible for managing classified forests, many of which have been illegally cleared for cocoa. OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves) — manages national parks and protected areas. CNS (Comité National de Surveillance) — the national committee monitoring child labor in cocoa, coordinated with the International Cocoa Initiative (ICI). ANADER — the national rural development support agency providing agricultural extension services.

Major cocoa traders operating in Côte d'Ivoire: Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam International, Sucden. These companies intermediate the majority of Ivorian cocoa exports. Cocoa and Forests Initiative (CFI) — a public-private partnership between cocoa-producing countries (Côte d'Ivoire, Ghana) and leading chocolate companies to end deforestación in cocoa supply chains. The Conseil du Café-Cacao (CCC) — the Ivorian cocoa board that sets farmgate prices and regulates the sector.

CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao) — the government body regulating Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa and coffee sectors, setting minimum farmgate prices, managing quality standards, and overseeing exports. MINEDD (Ministère de l'Environnement et du Développement Durable) — the environment ministry responsible for forest protection, climate policy, and ambiental regulation.

Assemblée nationale de Côte d'Ivoire — unicameral parliament with 255 members. Citizens can contact deputies through the official Assembly website. Sénat — the upper house (99 members, established 2020). ICCO (International Cocoa Organization) — the intergovernmental body coordinating global cocoa policy, headquartered in Abidjan. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR, 2023/1115) — requires EU importers to verify that commodities including cocoa are not linked to deforestación after December 2020.

CL.s: Upper-middle-income economía ($330.3B PIB) with massive minería sector (16.23% of PIB). World's largest copper producer (~27% of global supply) and second-largest lithium producer. Strong institutions and democratic governance. Renewable energy leader in Latin America (solar in Atacama).

sectors.w: Chile's copper and lithium minería depletes water resources in the Atacama Desert (one of Earth's driest regions). Cuando por mina water consumption data is published alongside production revenue, W se vuelve computable from minería output data.
bp: Implementar water pricing that reflects true scarcity in minería regions. Cuando miners pay the real cost of water extraction in hyper-arid zones, el interés superpuesto en water-intensive extraction methods se debilita — desalination and dry processing become economically preferred.

Mandatory por mina water balance, tailings chemistry, and emisiones disclosure. Chile's SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) already collects ambiental data — making it publicly accessible per mine makes W computable.
Parcialmente rompible. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes. However, dry processing, desalination, and tailings reprocessing can significantly reduce water dependence and waste generation.
a: Report ambiental violations to SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente)
Document water rights violations — report to DGA (Dirección General de Aguas)
Use Chile's denunciante law (Ley 21.015) and ambiental complaint channels (denuncia ambiental) for protected reporting
CODELCO (state copper company): Transition to desalinated water for all operations
Adoptar Science Based Targets for emisiones reduction across minería operations
Implementar ICMM Mining Principles including tailings management standards
SMA: Increase ambiental monitoring frequency and enforce water extraction limits at minería operations
Sernageomin: Fortalecer tailings dam safety standards (Chile has 740+ tailings facilities)
Ministry of Mining: Implementar the National Lithium Strategy with binding ambiental standards
Contacte a su Diputado or Senador in the Congreso Nacional para exigir mandatory water-neutral minería operations through desalination requirements
Support the Escazú Agreement on ambiental access rights in Latin America (Chile has not ratified)
Advocate for OECD Due Diligence Guidance enforcement for minería supply chains
Presionar por international coordination on critical mineral sustainability standards through the Minerals Security Partnership
i: SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) — Chile's ambiental enforcement agency, responsible for monitoring compliance with ambiental permits (RCA — Resolución de Calificación Ambiental). SMA can impose fines up to $8.2M and order facility closure. DGA (Dirección General de Aguas) — the national water authority managing water rights and monitoring extraction. SEA (Servicio de Evaluación Ambiental) — conducts ambiental impact assessments for minería projects.

CODELCO (Corporación Nacional del Cobre) — Chile's estatal copper company and the world's largest copper producer. CODELCO operates Chuquicamata, El Teniente, and other major mines. SQM (Sociedad Química y Minera de Chile) — one of the world's largest lithium producers, operating in the Salar de Atacama. Albemarle — US company operating lithium extraction in the Salar de Atacama alongside SQM. BHP, Anglo American — major international copper minería companies operating in Chile.

Sernageomin (Servicio Nacional de Geología y Minería) — the national geological and minería service, responsible for mine safety, tailings dam oversight, and geological surveys. Chile has over 740 tailings facilities, the largest inventory in the world. Ministry of Mining — oversees minería policy including the 2023 National Lithium Strategy, which established state-led lithium development through public-private partnerships.

Congreso Nacional de Chile — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputadas y Diputados (155 members) and the Senado (50 members). Citizens can contact representatives through camara.cl and senado.cl. Chile is an OECD member with strong democratic institutions. Escazú Agreement — the Regional Agreement on Access to Information, Public Participation and Justice in Environmental Matters in Latin America and the Caribbean. Chile signed but has not ratified.

CM.s: Central African economía ($53.3B PIB) with oil production (2.94% combined rents) and significant agriculture (18.5% PIB — cocoa, palm oil, timber). Forest cover declining due to logging and agricultural expansion. Governance challenges and Anglophone crisis.

sectors.w: Cameroon's oil production is declining from mature fields. As extraction costs rise, the interés superpuesto between operators (Perenco, Addax) and government revenue se debilita — diversification to sustainable forestry and agriculture becomes economically rational.
bp: Redirect declining oil revenues to sustainable forest management and agricultural diversification. Cameroon's forests are a carbon sink worth more standing than cleared. Cuando REDD+ payments exceed marginal oil revenue, el interés superpuesto en continued extraction breaks.
Implementar EITI-standard per-block ambiental disclosure. Cameroon's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to ambiental data makes W computable.
No se puede romper by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report ambiental violations to MINEPDED (Ministry of Environment, Nature Protection and Sustainable Desarrollarment)
Document illegal logging or land seizures — report to international forestry monitoring organizations (Global Forest Watch)
Report through EITI Cameroon multi-stakeholder channels
Perenco, Addax/Sinopec: Implementar ambiental monitoring and emisiones reporting for all Cameroon operations
SNH: Desarrollar post-oil diversification strategy
Adoptar TCFD-aligned climate disclosure
MINMIDT (Ministry of Mines): Fortalecer ambiental conditions in production licenses
MINEPDED: Hacer cumplir ambiental impact assessment requirements
EITI Cameroon: Expand reporting to ambiental data
Contacte a su Député in the Assemblée nationale para exigir transparent management of declining oil revenues
Support CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) for forest conservation as alternative revenue
Abogar a través de CEMAC for regional ambiental standards
Presionar por EITI enhanced reporting
i: MINEPDED (Ministère de l'Environnement, de la Protection de la Nature et du Développement Durable) — Cameroon's environment ministry. SNH (Société Nationale des Hydrocarbures) — the national hydrocarbons company managing state interests in oil and gas. EITI Cameroon — member since 2007. Note: Whistleblower protections are limited.

Perenco — the largest oil producer in Cameroon. Addax Petroleum (Sinopec subsidiary) — operates offshore concessions. SNH manages the state's petroleum interests and holds equity in production sharing contracts.

MINMIDT (Ministère des Mines, de l'Industrie et du Développement Technologique) — oversees minería and industrial policy. CONAC (Commission Nationale Anti-Corruption) — Cameroon's anti-corruption commission.

Assemblée nationale du Cameroun — 180 members. Sénat — 100 members. Note: Cameroon's democratic institutions are constrained under long-ruling President Paul Biya (in power since 1982). International channels: CEMAC, African Union, CAFI.

CO.s: Major Latin American economía ($418.8B PIB) with significant oil (3.42% rents) and coal (0.73% rents) production. Ecopetrol is the largest company. Biodiversity hotspot (most biodiverse country per square kilometer). 2016 peace agreement with FARC opened new governance challenges in former conflict zones.

sectors.w: Colombia's current administration (Petro, 2022-) has announced a policy of no new oil exploration licenses. If maintained, el interés superpuesto en fossil fuel expansion breaks as existing fields deplete. Colombia's renewable potential (hydro, solar, wind) provides viable alternatives.
bp: Maintain and implement the no-new-exploration-licenses policy while investing transition revenues in energía renovable. Cuando energy investment flows shift from fossil to renewable, el interés superpuesto en continued exploration breaks.

Mandatory por campo emisiones disclosure for all Ecopetrol and international operator fields. Colombia's ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) already collects production data — extending to emisiones makes W computable.
No se puede romper by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report ambiental violations to ANLA (Autoridad Nacional de Licencias Ambientales)
Document community health impacts near oil operations — report to Defensoría del Pueblo
Report through Ecopetrol's ethics line or international operator compliance channels
Ecopetrol: Accelerate energy transition strategy with binding renewable targets
Implementar TCFD-aligned reporting across all operations
Invertir en energía renovable in post-conflict zones to provide economic alternatives
MinAmbiente: Fortalecer ambiental licensing for extractive projects in biodiverse regions
CREG: Reformar energy tariffs to accelerate energía renovable deployment
ANH: Implementar por campo emisiones monitoring
Contacte a su Representante or Senador in the Congreso to support the energy transition framework legislation
Fortalecer Colombia's NDC with binding sectoral emisiones reduction targets
Leverage OECD membership to advance ambiental governance standards
Support the Leticia Pact for Amazon conservation as alternative economic model
i: ANLA (Autoridad Nacional de Licencias Ambientales) — Colombia's ambiental licensing authority, responsible for ambiental impact assessment and compliance monitoring for extractive projects. Defensoría del Pueblo — the national human rights ombudsman. Contraloría General de la República — the national audit office overseeing public resource management including oil revenues.

Ecopetrol — Colombia's largest company (88.5% estatal), responsible for most domestic oil production. Ecopetrol has announced energy transition targets but continues significant upstream investment. ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) — manages exploration and production contracts.

MinAmbiente (Ministerio de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sostenible) — Colombia's environment ministry. CREG (Comisión de Regulación de Energía y Gas) — the regulador energéticoy commission. CAR (Corporaciones Autónomas Regionales) — regional ambiental authorities.

Congreso de la República de Colombia — bicameral legislature: Cámara de Representantes (188 members) and Senado (108 members). Contact through camara.gov.co and senado.gov.co. Colombia is an OECD member (2020) with strong democratic institutions. The 2016 Peace Agreement (Acuerdo de Paz) created new governance frameworks for former conflict zones that intersect with extractive industry regulation.

CR.s: Upper-middle-income economía ($95.4B PIB) with 98%+ renewable electricity generation. No military since 1948. Services-dominant (68.8% PIB). Leader in ecotourism and ambiental policy. Low direct fossil fuel extraction. Main PST exposure through agricultural monoculture (pineapple, banana, palm oil).

sectors.w: Costa Rica's pineapple and banana monoculture uses intensive pesticide application that contaminates waterways. Cuando per-farm pesticide runoff and water contamination data is published alongside export revenue, W se vuelve computable.
bp: Reformar agricultural export incentives to reward biodiversity-positive farming. Cuando export tax benefits require ambiental certification, the interés superpuesto between monoculture producers and exporters in chemical-intensive production se debilita.

Mandatory per-farm water quality monitoring downstream of monoculture operations. Costa Rica's AyA (water utility) and SINAC already collect water data — linking to agricultural output makes W computable.
Parcialmente rompible. Tropical monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity. However, Costa Rica's Payment for Environmental Services (PSA) program demonstrates viable alternatives.
a: Report pesticide violations to SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado)
Document water contamination — report to MINAE (Ministry of Environment) or AyA
Use Costa Rica's labor protections and ambiental complaint mechanisms
Agricultural exporters (Del Monte, Dole, Chiquita): Adoptar pesticide reduction targets
Invertir en organic certification and agroforestry transitions
Implementar watershed-level ambiental monitoring
MAG (Ministry of Agriculture): Reformar export incentives to require ambiental certification
MINAE: Fortalecer pesticide regulations and enforce water quality standards near monoculture areas
SETENA: Require cumulative ambiental impact assessments for monoculture expansion
Contacte a su Diputado in the Asamblea Legislativa para exigir pesticide reduction legislation for monoculture agriculture
Support the Escazú Agreement implementation (Costa Rica was an early advocate)
Leverage Costa Rica's ambiental reputation to advance international agricultural sustainability standards
Presionar por green trade provisions in bilateral agreements
i: SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado) — the state phytosanitary service regulating pesticide use. MINAE (Ministerio de Ambiente y Energía) — the environment and energy ministry. AyA (Instituto Costarricense de Acueductos y Alcantarillados) — the national water utility. SETENA (Secretaría Técnica Nacional Ambiental) — conducts ambiental impact assessments.

Costa Rica hosts operations of major fruit multinationals: Del Monte, Dole, Chiquita. CORBANA (Corporación Bananera Nacional) — the national banana corporation coordinating the sector. CANAPEP (Cámara Nacional de Productores y Exportadores de Piña) — the pineapple producers' association.

MAG (Ministerio de Agricultura y Ganadería) — Costa Rica's agriculture ministry. The PSA (Pago por Servicios Ambientales) program — Costa Rica's pioneering payment for ambiental services program, which pays landowners to maintain forest cover, funded partly by a fuel tax.

Asamblea Legislativa de Costa Rica — unicameral parliament with 57 members. Citizens can contact representatives through asamblea.go.cr. Costa Rica is one of Latin America's strongest democracies and a global ambiental leader. The Escazú Agreement — Costa Rica was one of the first signatories of this regional agreement on ambiental access rights.

CU.s: Command economía ($107.4B PIB, though estimates vary due to dual currency system). Services-dominant (73.5% PIB, driven by healthcare, education, tourism). Minimal resource extraction. Economic crisis exacerbated by US embargo and collapse of Venezuelan oil subsidies.

sectors.w: Cuba's sugar industry collapsed after Soviet subsidies ended, but remaining agriculture (state farms, cooperatives) relies on monoculture patterns. Cuba's organic farming necessity (post-Soviet fertilizer shortage) demonstrates alternative models. Making soil health data visible breaks system independence.
bp: Expand the urban agriculture and organoponics model that Cuba developed out of necessity. Cuando organic production meets domestic food needs more reliably than import-dependent monoculture, el interés superpuesto en conventional agriculture breaks.
Publish soil health, water quality, and biodiversity data from Cuba's extensive agricultural research system. Cuba's scientific institutions (INIFAT, ICA) generate data that could make W computable.
Parcialmente rompible. Cuba's experience with organic farming post-1991 shows that dependencia del sistema on chemical inputs can be dramatically reduced through agroecological methods.
a: Document ambiental degradation through academic research channels
Report through workplace union structures (CTC — Central de Trabajadores de Cuba)
Engage with international agricultural research partnerships
State agricultural enterprises: Scale organoponics and agroecological methods
AZCUBA: Diversify sugar industry toward bioenergy and specialty products
International partners: Support technology transfer for sustainable agriculture
MINAG: Expand support for agroecological transition
CITMA: Fortalecer ambiental monitoring of agricultural practices
INIFAT: Publish research on agroecological transition outcomes
Support FAO technical cooperation programs for Cuban agricultural sustainability
Abogar a través de CELAC for regional agricultural knowledge exchange
Presionar por relaxation of US embargo restrictions on agricultural technology transfer
Support South-South cooperation on agroecological methods developed in Cuba
i: Note: Cuba is a estado de partido único. Independent sociedad civil, media, and labor organizations are not permitted. Reporting channels operate within state structures. CTC (Central de Trabajadores de Cuba) — the state-controlled trade union federation. INIFAT (Instituto Nacional de Investigaciones Fundamentales en Agricultura Tropical) — the national agricultural research institute. CITMA (Ministerio de Ciencia, Tecnología y Medio Ambiente) — the science and environment ministry.

AZCUBA (Empresa Azucarera) — Cuba's state sugar enterprise. State agricultural enterprises and UBPCs (Unidades Básicas de Producción Cooperativa) — cooperatives managing most agricultural production. Cuba's urban agriculture program is one of the world's most successful, producing significant food in cities through organoponics.

MINAG (Ministerio de la Agricultura) — Cuba's agriculture ministry. CITMA — oversees ambiental policy and scientific research. The Asociación Nacional de Agricultores Pequeños (ANAP) — the national small farmers' association, which has been a key partner in agroecological transition.

Note: Cuba's Asamblea Nacional del Poder Popular (605 members) is the legislature but functions within a one-party system. International engagement channels: FAO (Cuba has received recognition for reducing hunger), CELAC (Community of Latin American and Caribbean States), NAM (Non-Aligned Movement). Cuba's agroecological experience is studied globally as a model for post-petrochemical agriculture.

CY.s: Small EU island economía ($37.6B PIB) with dominant services sector (76.5% PIB, driven by financial services, shipping, and tourism). No significant resource extraction. Post-2013 banking crisis reforms strengthened financial regulation. Division between Republic of Cyprus and northern territory.

sectors.w: Cyprus's financial services sector has historically facilitated capital flows from Russia and other jurisdictions. EU AML directives and beneficial ownership transparencia requirements make the welfare impact of opaque financial intermediation computable.
bp: Full implementation of EU Anti-Money Laundering Directives and beneficial ownership transparencia. Cuando financial secrecy is eliminated, the interés superpuesto between Cypriot service providers and clients seeking opacity se debilita.

Mandatory publication of Country-by-Country Reporting (CbCR) data for entities registered in Cyprus. EU ATAD and DAC6 frameworks provide the mechanism — making tax planning structures visible breaks system independence.
Parcialmente rompible. Financial intermediation can relocate — but Cyprus's miembro de la UEship, common law heritage, and timezone advantages create switching costs.
a: Report suspicious transactions to MOKAS (Unit for Combating Money Laundering)
Document beneficial ownership concealment — report to CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission)
Use EU Whistleblower Directive protections (transposed 2022)
Implementar enhanced due diligence for all high-risk client relationships
Adoptar full beneficial ownership transparencia ahead of EU AMLD6 requirements
Integrate ESG criteria into financial advisory services
CySEC: Fortalecer beneficial ownership verification requirements
CBC: Hacer cumplir enhanced AML due diligence for correspondent banking relationships
ICPAC (Institute of Certified Public Accountants): Ensure audit firms enforce transparencia standards
Contacte a su Βουλευτής in the Βουλή των Αντιπροσώπων (House of Representatives) para exigir full implementation of EU AML directives
Support EU AMLD6 negotiations for enhanced transparencia requirements
Advocate for OECD BEPS Inclusive Framework strengthening
Presionar por EU-wide beneficial ownership registry interconnection
i: MOKAS (Μονάδα Καταπολέμησης Αδικημάτων Συγκάλυψης / Unit for Combating Money Laundering) — Cyprus's financial intelligence unit. CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission) — the securities and financial markets regulator. CBC (Central Bank of Cyprus) — the prudential supervisor of banks. Cyprus transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

Cyprus hosts approximately 40 licensed banks and numerous investment firms. The Cyprus Investment Firms sector manages significant cross-border capital flows. CIFA (Cyprus Investment Funds Association) — the industry association representing investment fund managers.

CySEC — responsible for securities regulation and investment firm supervision. CBC (Central Bank of Cyprus) — conducts banking supervision and AML compliance monitoring. ICPAC — the professional body for accountants and auditors in Cyprus.

Βουλή των Αντιπροσώπων (House of Representatives) — unicameral parliament with 56 elected members (80 seats including 24 reserved for Turkish Cypriot community). Contact through parliament.cy. Cyprus is an miembro de la UE with robust democratic institutions. Moneyval (Council of Europe's Committee of Experts on the Evaluation of Anti-Money Laundering Measures) evaluates Cyprus's AML framework.

CZ.s: Central European industrial economía ($347B PIB) with strong manufacturing (19.9% PIB, automotive-dominated). Small coal sector (lignite in northern Bohemia). miembro de la UE. Significant nuclear energy capacity (Dukovany, Temelín). Servicios al 60.2% PIB.

sectors.w: Czechia's lignite minería is concentrated in the Ústí nad Labem region (Sokolovská uhelná, Severní energetická). EU ETS carbon pricing makes Czech lignite uncompetitive — the interés superpuesto between mine operators and local employment breaks when carbon costs exceed revenue.
bp: Align with EU ETS carbon pricing and eliminate free allowances for Czech lignite power. Cuando plants pay the full carbon price, lignite electricity becomes more expensive than alternatives and the interés superpuesto breaks.
Mandatory por planta emisiones and health-impact reporting. Czech lignite plants cause significant air pollution — publishing health costs per MWh makes W computable.
No se puede romper. Lignite combustion releases CO₂ and pollutants by physical process.
a: Report emisiones violations to ČIŽP (Czech Environmental Inspectorate)
Document health impacts in minería communities — report to regional public health authorities
Use the Czech denunciante protection law (zákon o ochraně oznamovatelů, 2023)
ČEZ (majority estatal): Accelerate coal phase-out timeline and energía renovable investment
Sev.en Energy: Desarrollar mine closure and site remediation plans
Invertir en nuclear energy expansion as baseload alternative
ERÚ: Reformar electricity tariffs to reflect carbon costs
MŽP: Fortalecer air quality enforcement in minería regions
Czech Coal Commission: Implementar recommendations on coal phase-out by 2033
Contacte a su Poslanec or Senátor in the Parlament para exigir a binding coal phase-out timeline with just transition measures
Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Ústí nad Labem and Moravian-Silesian regions
Advocate for accelerated EU ETS reform
Presionar por Territorial Just Transition Plans with concrete retraining programs
i: ČIŽP (Česká inspekce životního prostředí / Czech Environmental Inspectorate) — the enforcement agency for ambiental law, conducting inspections and imposing sanciones. MŽP (Ministerstvo životního prostředí / Ministry of the Environment) — oversees ambiental policy. Zákon o ochraně oznamovatelů (Act on Protection of Whistleblowers, 2023) — Czech transposition of EU Whistleblower Directive.

ČEZ Group — Czechia's largest energy company (70% estatal), operating coal, nuclear, and renewable power plants. Sev.en Energy — the largest private lignite minería and power company. Sokolovská uhelná — operates lignite mines in the Sokolov basin.

ERÚ (Energetický regulační úřad / Energy Regulatory Office) — the independent regulador energético. Czech Coal Commission (Uhelná komise) — established to recommend a coal phase-out date. Recommended 2033, but implementation timelines remain debated.

Parlament České republiky — bicameral: Poslanecká sněmovna (200 members) and Senát (81 members). Contact through psp.cz and senat.cz. EU Just Transition Fund — Czechia is eligible for significant funding for coal-dependent regions.

DK.s: Advanced Nordic economía ($424.5B PIB) with global leadership in wind energy (Vestas, Ørsted). Intensive livestock agriculture (28M pigs for 5.8M people) creates significant nitrogen pollution. Servicios al 63.5% PIB. World's most ambitious climate law (70% reduction by 2030).

sectors.w: Denmark's intensive pig farming generates nitrogen pollution visible in marine dead zones (inner Danish waters, Baltic Sea). EU Nitrates Directive compliance data makes W computable from farm-level nitrogen balance sheets.
bp: Reformar agricultural subsidies to penalize nitrogen surplus per hectare. Cuando the interés superpuesto between livestock producers and feed importers is broken by internalizing the nitrogen externality, intensive livestock becomes unprofitable without subsidy.

Mandatory farm-level nitrogen and phosphorus balance disclosure. Denmark already has one of Europe's most detailed agricultural data systems — extending to mandatory public disclosure makes W computable.

Parcialmente rompible. Intensive livestock inherently concentrates nutrients beyond ecosystem absorption. However, Denmark's leadership in biogas from manure demonstrates dependencia del sistema can be reduced through circular nutrient management.
a: Report nitrogen limit violations to Miljøstyrelsen (Danish Environmental Protection Agency)
Document animal welfare violations — report to Fødevarestyrelsen
Use Danish denunciante law (Lov om beskyttelse af denunciantee, 2021)
Danish Crown, Arla: Invertir en plant-based protein alternatives
Implementar nitrogen-neutral farming targets across supply chains
Adoptar Scope 3 agricultural emisiones reporting
Miljøministeriet: Tighten nitrogen quotas for marine ecosystem recovery
Landbrugsstyrelsen: Reformar CAP eco-scheme payments to reward nitrogen-negative farming
Implementar the 2021 agreement on green transition of Danish agriculture (55-65% emisiones reduction by 2030)
Contacte a su member of the Folketing para exigir enforcement of agricultural nitrogen reduction targets
Support EU-wide mandatory nitrogen balance disclosure
Advocate for carbon border adjustment on imported animal feed from deforestación regions
Presionar por Baltic Sea Action Plan implementation through HELCOM
i: Miljøstyrelsen — responsible for ambiental regulation including agricultural emisiones and water quality. Fødevarestyrelsen (Danish Veterinary and Food Administration) — oversees food safety and animal welfare. Danish Whistleblower Act (2021) — transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937.
Danish Crown — one of Europe's largest meat processors (farmer cooperative). Arla Foods — multinational dairy cooperative headquartered in Denmark. DLG Group — major agricultural input cooperative.

Miljøministeriet (Ministry of Environment) — ambiental policy including agricultural regulation. Landbrugsstyrelsen (Danish Agricultural Agency) — administers CAP payments. The 2021 green agriculture agreement is one of Europe's most ambitious.

Folketinget — unicameral parliament (179 members). Contact through ft.dk. Klimaloven (Climate Act, 2020) — legally binding 70% emisiones reduction target by 2030. HELCOM (Helsinki Commission) — the governing body of the Convention on the Protection of the Marine Environment of the Baltic Sea Area.

DO.s: Caribbean's largest economía ($124.3B PIB) with tourism, manufacturing (free trade zones), and minería (2.04% PIB — Pueblo Viejo gold mine, one of the world's largest). Servicios al 59.8% PIB. Climate vulnerable — hurricanes and sea level rise.

sectors.w: Pueblo Viejo gold mine (Barrick Gold) generates cyanide and mercury contamination risks. Cuando por mina water quality and health data is published alongside production revenue, W se vuelve computable.
bp: Mandate ambiental bonds that fully internalize mine closure and remediation costs. Cuando minería companies pre-fund cleanup, el interés superpuesto en extraction without ambiental accountability se debilita.
Mandatory water quality monitoring and health-impact disclosure per mine. Publishing downstream contamination data makes W computable from minería revenue.
Parcialmente rompible. Open-pit minería disturbs landscapes. However, improved tailings management and water treatment can reduce dependencia del sistema.
a: Report ambiental violations to the Ministry of Environment and Natural Resources
Document community health impacts — report to health authorities
Report through Barrick Gold's compliance channels
Barrick Gold (Pueblo Viejo): Implementar GISTM tailings management standards
Adoptar full water quality disclosure for all operations
Invertir en community development programs per minería code requirements
Ministry of Energy and Mines: Fortalecer ambiental conditions in minería concessions
Hacer cumplir Mining Law 146-71 ambiental provisions
Publish minería sector ambiental compliance data
Contacte a su Diputado or Senador in the Congreso Nacional para exigir mandatory ambiental bonds for minería operations
Support EITI membership for the Dominican Republic
Abogar a través de SICA for regional minería ambiental standards
Presionar por minería revenue transparencia
i: Ministerio de Medio Ambiente y Recursos Naturales — the environment ministry. Dirección General de Minería — the minería directorate under the Ministry of Energy and Mines. Defensor del Pueblo — the national ombudsman.
Barrick Gold — operates Pueblo Viejo, one of the world's largest gold mines (JV with Newmont). The mine produces ~800,000 ounces of gold annually.
Ministerio de Energía y Minas — oversees minería policy. Ley 146-71 (Mining Law) — governs minería concessions and ambiental obligations.
Congreso Nacional — bicameral: Cámara de Diputados (190 members) and Senado (32 members). The Dominican Republic is a presidential democracia.

DZ.s: Major oil and gas producer ($269.3B PIB) with 22.46% combined oil and gas rents. Sonatrach is Africa's largest company. Economy heavily dependent on hydrocarbon exports (90%+ of export revenue). Agricultura al 14% PIB (primarily wheat and dates). Authoritarian governance.

sectors.w: Algeria's oil production is declining from mature Hassi Messaoud and Hassi R'Mel fields. As domestic energy consumption grows (population of 45M+), the exportable surplus shrinks. Cuando production costs exceed export revenue minus domestic consumption, el interés superpuesto en continued expansion breaks.
bp: Redirect hydrocarbon revenues to solar energy (Algeria has the Sahara — one of Earth's highest solar irradiance zones). Cuando solar electricity is cheaper than burning domestically-consumed gas, el interés superpuesto en gas-fired power generation breaks.

Implementar por campo emisiones disclosure for Sonatrach operations. Algeria's massive gas flaring (visible from satellite) makes ambiental damage measurable — publishing this data makes W computable.
No se puede romper by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report ambiental violations through Sonatrach internal channels
Document gas flaring data — report to Ministry of Energy and Mines
Report concerns to international partner compliance systems (Eni, TotalEnergies, Equinor)
Sonatrach: Desarrollar solar energy division using Saharan land bank
Implementar flaring reduction program
International partners (Eni, TotalEnergies): Apply global emisiones standards to Algerian operations
ALNAFT: Fortalecer ambiental conditions in exploration and production contracts
CREG: Accelerate domestic energía renovable deployment (the PNER 2030 targets 22GW solar)
ARH: Hacer cumplir flaring reduction regulations
Abogar a través de OPEC for managed production decline aligned with Paris Agreement
Support Mediterranean Solar Plan and Desertec-style initiatives for Saharan solar export
Presionar por EITI membership for Algeria
Leverage Algeria's gas supply role to Europe for energy transition dialogue
i: Sonatrach — Algeria's state oil and gas company, Africa's largest company by revenue. Sonatrach controls all upstream and downstream petroleum operations. Note: Algeria has limited press freedom and no comprehensive denunciante protection. Independent reporting is constrained.
Sonatrach operates the world's longest natural gas pipelines (Transmed to Italy, MEG to Spain). Eni, TotalEnergies, Equinor, Occidental — major international partners in Algerian upstream operations.

ALNAFT (Agence Nationale pour la Valorisation des Ressources en Hydrocarbures) — the hydrocarbons regulatory agency managing licensing. CREG (Commission de Régulation de l'Électricité et du Gaz) — the electricity and gas regulatory commission. ARH (Autorité de Régulation des Hydrocarbures) — the hydrocarbons sector regulator.

Note: Algeria is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée populaire nationale (407 members) and Conseil de la Nation (174 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International channels: OPEC membership, Mediterranean dialogue (Union for the Mediterranean), African Union, Arab League. Algeria's strategic gas supply position (supplier to Italy, Spain via pipeline) gives it leverage in energy transition negotiations.

EC.s: Oil-dependent economía ($124.7B PIB) with 6.41% oil rents. PetroEcuador manages state interests. Extraordinary biodiversity (Galápagos, Amazon basin). The 2008 Constitution grants 'rights of nature' (Pachamama). Agricultura al 9.5% PIB (bananas, shrimp, cacao).

sectors.w: Ecuador's 2023 referendum voted to ban oil extraction in Yasuní National Park (one of the world's most biodiverse areas). This democratic decision to forgo extraction directly breaks the interés superpuesto. Extending this precedent to other sensitive areas accelerates the transition.
bp: Implementar the Yasuní referendum result (ban extraction in ITT block) and extend the precedent to other ecologically sensitive areas. Cuando democratic decisions override extraction interests, the interés superpuesto breaks through civic action.

Mandate per-block ambiental impact disclosure for all PetroEcuador and private operator fields. Ecuador's ambiental data (Amazon deforestación, water contamination from legacy Texaco/Chevron operations) makes W measurable.
No se puede romper by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report ambiental violations to MAATE (Ministry of Environment, Water and Ecological Transition)
Document community health impacts from oil operations — report to the Defensoría del Pueblo
Use Ecuador's Constitutional protections for ambiental defenders
PetroEcuador: Desarrollar transition plan to phase out operations in ecologically sensitive areas
Implementar TCFD-aligned disclosure
Invertir en remediation of legacy contamination (Lago Agrio)
MAATE: Hacer cumplir Yasuní referendum result and strengthen ambiental licensing
ARCERNNR: Reformar energy regulation to accelerate renewable deployment
SHE (Secretaría de Hidrocarburos del Ecuador): Align licensing with rights of nature framework
Contacte a su Asambleísta in the Asamblea Nacional para exigir implementation of the Yasuní referendum and extension to other sensitive areas
Leverage Ecuador's rights of nature framework in international climate negotiations
Support the Amazon Cooperation Treaty Organization (ACTO) for regional ambiental governance
Presionar por debt-for-nature swaps to finance the transition away from oil dependence
i: MAATE (Ministerio del Ambiente, Agua y Transición Ecológica) — Ecuador's environment ministry. Defensoría del Pueblo — the national ombudsman. Ecuador's 2008 Constitution (Articles 71-74) grants legally enforceable rights to nature (Pachamama), a global first.

EP PetroEcuador — the state oil company. PetroAmazonas EP — manages upstream operations in the Amazon basin. The Lago Agrio case (Chevron/Texaco) — one of the world's largest ambiental lawsuits over oil contamination in the Ecuadorian Amazon.
ARCERNNR (Agencia de Regulación y Control de Energía y Recursos Naturales No Renovables) — the energy and natural resources regulatory agency.

Asamblea Nacional del Ecuador — unicameral parliament (137 members). Contact through asambleanacional.gob.ec. Ecuador's rights of nature framework (2008 Constitution) and the 2023 Yasuní referendum are globally significant legal precedents for ambiental governance.

EE.s: Advanced digital economía ($43.1B PIB) with unique oil shale dependence (0.96% oil rents from kukersite extraction). Services-dominant (65.7% PIB). EU and NATO member. World leader in e-governance. Oil shale power generation is Estonia's largest source of CO₂ emisiones.

sectors.w: Estonia's oil shale sector is concentrated in Ida-Viru county (Eesti Energia/Enefit). EU ETS carbon costs make oil shale electricity the most expensive fossil option. Cuando carbon pricing fully applies, the interés superpuesto between shale operators and electricity consumers breaks.
bp: Full EU ETS alignment without compensation. Oil shale electricity already costs more than wind in the Baltic. Cuando subsidies and free allowances are eliminated, el interés superpuesto en oil shale generation breaks — renewables are cheaper.
Mandatory por planta lifecycle emisiones and health-impact disclosure for oil shale processing. Estonia's detailed ambiental monitoring makes W computable.
No se puede romper. Oil shale processing releases CO₂, particulate matter, and phenolic wastewater by physical and chemical process.
a: Report ambiental violations to Keskkonnaamet (Environmental Board)
Document health impacts in Ida-Viru communities
Use Estonian denunciante law (rikkumisest teavitaja kaitse seadus, 2022)
Eesti Energia/Enefit: Accelerate transition from oil shale to energía renovable
Invertir en Ida-Viru economic diversification
Adoptar binding emisiones reduction targets
Konkurentsiamet: Reformar electricity tariffs to reflect full ambiental costs
Ministry of Environment: Fortalecer oil shale processing ambiental standards
Implementar just transition plan for Ida-Viru county
Contacte a su member of the Riigikogu para exigir a binding oil shale phase-out timeline
Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Ida-Viru region
Advocate for Baltic energy market integration to enable energía renovable scaling
Presionar por HELCOM enforcement on Baltic Sea pollution from oil shale waste
i: Keskkonnaamet (Environmental Board) — Estonia's ambiental enforcement and permitting authority. Keskkonnaministeerium (Ministry of the Environment) — oversees ambiental policy. Estonia transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

Eesti Energia (Enefit) — the estatal energy company and dominant oil shale operator. Enefit operates the Narva power plants (the largest oil shale power plants in the world) and oil shale processing facilities.

Konkurentsiamet (Competition Authority) — also serves as Estonia's regulador energético. Ida-Viru county hosts ~90% of Estonia's oil shale operations and faces significant economic transition challenges.

Riigikogu — Estonia's unicameral parliament (101 members). Contact through riigikogu.ee. Estonia's Climate Ambition target includes significant emisiones reduction from the energy sector. EU Just Transition Fund — Estonia receives JTF funding for Ida-Viru transition.

EG.s: Largest Arab economía by population ($389.1B PIB). Oil and gas rents at 5.04%. Major gas producer (Zohr field, Mediterranean). Agricultura al 13.7% PIB. High military expenditure. Hosted COP27. Suez Canal provides strategic global trade leverage.

sectors.w: Egypt's Zohr gas field (discovered 2015) transformed Egypt from gas importer to exporter. COP27 presidency created domestic pressure for emisiones transparencia. Per-field emisiones disclosure makes W computable.
bp: Redirect gas export revenue to solar energy development. Egypt's solar irradiance is among the world's highest. Cuando solar electricity undercuts gas-fired generation domestically, el interés superpuesto en gas-for-domestic-power se debilita.

Implementar por campo emisiones disclosure for Zohr and other major fields. Egypt's COP27 commitments create institutional pressure — making lifecycle emisiones visible per unit of production makes W computable.
No se puede romper by policy alone. Gas combustion releases CO₂.
a: Report ambiental violations to EEAA (Egyptian Environmental Affairs Agency)
Document emisiones data through international partner channels (Eni, BP)
Report through EGPC internal compliance mechanisms
Eni (Zohr operator): Implementar emisiones reduction targets for Egyptian operations

i: EEAA (Egyptian Environmental Affairs Agency) — Egypt's ambiental regulatory body. EGPC (Egyptian General Petroleum Corporation) — the state oil and gas company. EGAS (Egyptian Natural Gas Holding Company) — manages natural gas operations. Note: Egypt has severe restrictions on sociedad civil and press freedom. Whistleblower protections are minimal.
Eni — operates Zohr, the largest gas field in the Mediterranean. BP — major operator in the West Nile Delta. EGPC and EGAS manage state interests in all petroleum operations.

Ministry of Petroleum and Mineral Resources — oversees hydrocarbons policy. NREA (New and Renewable Energy Authority) — responsible for energía renovable development. Egypt's Integrated Sustainable Energy Strategy targets 42% renewable electricity by 2035.

Note: Egypt under President el-Sisi is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The House of Representatives (Majlis al-Nuwab, 596 members) does not function as an independent democratic institution. International channels: Arab League, African Union (Egypt hosted AU in 2019), Union for the Mediterranean, COP27 legacy initiatives.

ET.s: Africa's second-most-populous country ($149.7B PIB). Agriculture dominates (34.8% PIB, primarily coffee, teff, livestock). Rapid industrialization drive (industrial parks). Major hydropower (GERD — Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam). Post-civil war recovery.

sectors.w: Ethiopia's agricultural expansion is the primary driver of deforestación (fr=5.6% PIB equivalent in forest rents). Cuando per-farm ambiental data (tree cover, soil carbon) is linked to agricultural output, W se vuelve computable.
bp: Scale Ethiopia's Productive Safety Net Program (PSNP) to include ambiental conditionality. Cuando agricultural support payments require soil conservation and tree planting, el interés superpuesto en clearing forest for short-term cultivation se debilita.

Satellite-based monitoring of forest cover and degradación del suelo published at woreda level. Ethiopia's Green Legacy Initiative provides political commitment — mandatory monitoring data makes W computable.

Parcialmente rompible. Subsistence agriculture on degraded land inherently affects soil and water systems. However, Ethiopia's traditional conservation agriculture (terracing, agroforestry) can reduce dependencia del sistema.
a: Report illegal deforestación to regional ambiental protection authorities
Document land grabbing or forced displacement — report to EHRC (Ethiopian Human Rights Commission)
Report through agricultural development agent (DA) networks at kebele level

Agricultural investment companies: Adoptar ambiental impact monitoring
Support Green Legacy Initiative reforestation targets

EFCCC: Fortalecer forest protection enforcement

Abogar a través de African Union (headquartered in Addis Ababa) for continental agricultural sustainability standards
Presionar por international climate finance for Ethiopia's agricultural transformation
Support Nile Basin Initiative cooperation on sustainable watershed management
i: Regional ambiental protection authorities — Ethiopia's federal system devolves ambiental management to regional states. EHRC (Ethiopian Human Rights Commission) — the national human rights institution. Desarrollarment Agents (DAs) — government agricultural extension workers deployed at kebele (ward) level throughout rural Ethiopia.

ECX (Ethiopian Commodity Exchange) — the commodities trading platform for coffee, sesame, and other agricultural products. Ethiopia is Africa's largest coffee producer and coffee is the country's largest export. Industrial parks development corporation — manages Ethiopia's growing manufacturing sector.

Ministry of Agriculture — oversees agricultural policy and the PSNP. EFCCC (Environment, Forest and Climate Change Commission) — Ethiopia's autoridad ambiental. Green Legacy Initiative — the government's flagship reforestation program targeting 20 billion seedlings.

Note: Ethiopia is a federal parlamentaria republic but democratic governance has been constrained by civil conflict (Tigray war 2020-2022). The House of Peoples' Representatives (547 members) is the lower house. International channels: African Union (HQ in Addis Ababa), Nile Basin Initiative, IGAD (Intergovernmental Authority on Desarrollarment), AFR100 (Africa's contribution to the Bonn Challenge on forest restoration).

FI.s: Advanced Nordic economía ($298.7B PIB) with strong forestry sector. Servicios al 62.2% PIB, manufacturing at 14.2%. High healthcare expenditure (10.47% PIB). Major pulp and paper producers (UPM, Stora Enso, Metsä Group). miembro de la UE with robust ambiental regulation.

sectors.w: Finland's forestry-driven monoculture (pine/spruce plantations replacing natural forests) reduces biodiversity and degrades peatland carbon stocks. Cuando per-harvest biodiversity and carbon data is published alongside timber revenue, W se vuelve computable.
bp: Reformar EU CAP and national forestry subsidies to reward biodiversity and carbon storage rather than timber volume. Cuando subsidies incentivize continuous-cover forestry over clear-cutting, el interés superpuesto en plantation monoculture se debilita.

Mandatory per-harvest ambiental data (biodiversity indicators, soil carbon, water quality). Finland's Metsähallitus (state forest enterprise) already collects extensive data — making it publicly accessible makes W computable.

Parcialmente rompible. Timber plantation inherently reduces biodiversity compared to natural forest. However, continuous-cover forestry and extended rotation lengths can significantly reduce dependencia del sistema.

Document biodiversity loss in logged areas — report to SYKE (Finnish Environment Institute)

i: ELY-keskus — regional authorities responsible for ambiental monitoring and enforcement. SYKE (Suomen ympäristökeskus / Finnish Environment Institute) — the national ambiental research and monitoring institute. Ilmoittajansuojelulaki (Whistleblower Protection Act, 2023) — Finnish transposition of EU Whistleblower Directive.

UPM-Kymmene — one of Finland's largest forestry companies. Stora Enso — Finnish-Swedish forestry and paper company. Metsä Group — cooperative of 100,000+ Finnish forest owners. Metsähallitus — state enterprise managing one-third of Finland's land area.

MMM (Maa- ja metsätalousministeriö) — the ministry responsible for agriculture and forestry policy. National Forest Strategy 2025 — Finland's strategic framework for forestry sector development. EU Nature Restoration Law — requires restoration of degraded ecosystems including forests.

Eduskunta (Parliament of Finland) — unicameral legislature with 200 members. Contact through eduskunta.fi. The Maa- ja metsätalousvaliokunta (Agriculture and Forestry Committee) handles forestry legislation. Finland's climate law targets carbon neutrality by 2035.

GA.s: Oil-dependent Central African economía ($20.9B PIB) with 15.83% combined oil and gas rents. 88% forest cover — one of the world's most forested countries. Low population (2.4M). Military coup August 2023 ended the Bongo family's 55-year rule.

sectors.w: Gabon's oil production is in terminal decline from mature fields (peaked at ~370,000 bpd in 1997, now ~200,000). As production falls, the interés superpuesto between operators and government revenue se debilita. Gabon's forests offer alternative revenue through carbon credits.
bp: Monetize Gabon's forest carbon stocks through REDD+ and voluntary carbon markets. Gabon received $17M from Norway for forest protection in 2021 — scaling this replaces declining oil revenue and breaks el interés superpuesto en extraction.
Implementar por campo emisiones disclosure for all operators (TotalEnergies, Perenco, Assala Energy). Making ambiental costs visible makes W computable.
No se puede romper by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂.
a: Report ambiental violations to ANPN (Agence Nationale des Parcs Nationaux)
Document oil spills or contamination — report through operator compliance channels

i: ANPN (Agence Nationale des Parcs Nationaux) — manages Gabon's 13 national parks (covering 11% of territory). GOC (Gabon Oil Company) — the state oil company. EITI Gabon — member since 2004. Note: Gabon is under military transition government since August 2023 coup.
TotalEnergies — the largest oil producer in Gabon. Perenco, Assala Energy — other major operators. GOC (Gabon Oil Company) — the state oil company.
Ministry of Hydrocarbons — oversees oil and gas policy. DGEPN (Direction Générale de l'Environnement et de la Protection de la Nature) — ambiental directorate.

Note: Gabon is under military transition government (Comité pour la Transition et la Restauration des Institutions, CTRI) since August 2023. Democratic institutions are suspended. International channels: CEMAC, CAFI, African Union, OPEC membership. Gabon's 2021 deal with Norway's sovereign wealth fund for forest protection is a model for forest-state climate finance.

GE.s: Small South Caucasus economía ($34.2B PIB) with minería sector (1.28% PIB, primarily manganese and copper). Services-dominant (62% PIB). candidato a la UE country (2023). Significant hydropower potential. Political tensions between pro-EU and pro-Russia orientations.

sectors.w: Georgia's manganese minería (Chiatura) and copper minería generate significant water pollution. EU accession ambiental alignment will require improved monitoring. Cuando water quality data is linked to minería output, W se vuelve computable.
bp: EU accession ambiental alignment. Cuando Georgia must meet EU ambiental standards for minería, el interés superpuesto en low-cost extraction without ambiental controls se debilita.
Mandatory por mina water quality and emisiones disclosure. Georgia's National Environmental Agency collects some data — extending to public disclosure makes W computable.
Parcialmente rompible. Mining disturbs landscapes, but improved tailings management can reduce dependencia del sistema.

i: NEA (National Environmental Agency) — responsible for ambiental monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. Ministry of Environmental Protection and Agriculture — oversees ambiental and agricultural policy. Georgia's Law on Freedom of Speech and Expression provides some protections for reporting.

Georgian Manganese — subsidiary of ERG (Eurasian Resources Group), operating the Chiatura manganese mines. RMG Copper — operates copper mines. Georgia's minería sector is primarily controlled by international investors.
National Agency of Mines — responsible for minería licensing and oversight. Georgia's EU Association Agreement (2014) includes provisions for ambiental alignment.

Sakartvelos Parlamenti (Parliament of Georgia) — unicameral legislature with 150 members. Contact through parliament.ge. Georgia is an candidato a la UE country (2023) and aspirant to full miembro de la UEship. EU Association Agreement — includes Deep and Comprehensive Free Trade Area (DCFTA) with ambiental provisions.

GH.s: West African economía ($82.3B PIB) with significant oil (4.06% rents), gold minería (5.17% mineral rents), and cocoa agriculture (20.9% ag share). One of Africa's most stable democracies. Debt crisis (2022 IMF bailout). Illegal artisanal gold minería (galamsey) is a major ambiental crisis.

sectors.w: Ghana's galamsey (illegal artisanal minería) crisis has contaminated water bodies across cocoa-growing regions. Cuando por mina water quality data is published and linked to gold production, W se vuelve computable. The galamsey problem is so severe that it threatens Ghana's water supply.
bp: Formalize artisanal minería with ambiental standards and alternative livelihood programs. Cuando legal small-scale minería provides comparable income with less ambiental damage, el interés superpuesto en illegal extraction se debilita.
Satellite-based water quality monitoring linked to minería areas. Ghana's Water Resources Commission already monitors water quality — extending to real-time public data makes W computable.
Parcialmente rompible. Mining disturbs landscapes. However, formalized artisanal minería with mercury-free processing (e.g., gravity separation) can significantly reduce dependencia del sistema.

i: IMCIM (Inter-Ministerial Committee on Illegal Mining) — established to coordinate government response to the galamsey crisis. WRC (Water Resources Commission) — manages Ghana's water resources and monitors water quality. Minerals Commission — the regulatory body for the minería sector, responsible for licensing and compliance monitoring.

Gold Fields — operates Tarkwa and Damang mines. Newmont — operates Ahafo and Akyem mines. AngloGold Ashanti — major gold minería company with Ghanaian operations. Ghana is Africa's largest gold producer.

EPA (Environmental Protection Agency of Ghana) — responsible for ambiental permitting, monitoring, and enforcement. Forestry Commission — manages Ghana's forest reserves, many of which are threatened by illegal minería. EITI Ghana — Ghana has been EITI compliant since 2010.

Parliament of Ghana — unicameral legislature with 275 members. Contact through parliament.gh. Ghana is a presidential democracia with peaceful transfers of power. Minamata Convention on Mercury — Ghana has ratified this treaty requiring phase-down of mercury use in artisanal gold minería.

GN.s: Resource-rich West African economía ($25B PIB) with massive bauxite reserves (world's largest — 7.4B tons). Agricultura al 31% PIB. Military junta since 2021 coup. Guinea supplies ~25% of global bauxite (the ore used to produce aluminum).

sectors.w: Guinea's bauxite minería displaces communities and contaminates water sources. As aluminum buyers face ESG pressure (EU CSRD, Apple/Tesla supply chain requirements), making por mina ambiental data visible breaks system independence.
bp: International supply chain due diligence (EU CSDDD) requiring ambiental and human rights compliance for bauxite. Cuando buyers must verify mine-level conditions, el interés superpuesto en low-cost, unregulated extraction se debilita.
Per-mine ambiental and community impact disclosure through EITI and supply chain traceability. Cuando downstream manufacturers can see the welfare cost per ton of bauxite, W se vuelve computable.
Parcialmente rompible. Open-pit bauxite minería inherently disturbs landscapes. However, progressive rehabilitation and dust suppression can reduce dependencia del sistema.

Document community displacement — report through international accountability mechanisms

Invertir en progressive rehabilitation of mined areas
Ministry of Mines: Hacer cumplir 2011 Mining Code ambiental provisions
CPDM (Centre de Promotion et de Développement Minier): Fortalecer ambiental monitoring capacity
EITI Guinea: Expand reporting to ambiental and community data

Support international accountability mechanisms for minería-affected communities
i: Note: Guinea is under military rule (CNRD — Comité National du Rassemblement et du Développement) since September 2021. Independent sociedad civil operates under severe constraints. Bureau Guinéen des Études et Évaluations Environnementales (BGEEE) — ambiental assessment authority. International monitoring: Human Rights Watch and Inclusive Desarrollarment International have documented impacts of bauxite minería on Guinean communities.

CBG (Compagnie des Bauxites de Guinée) — JV of Alcoa, Rio Tinto, and Dadco, one of the world's largest bauxite producers. SMB (Société Minière de Boké) — Chinese-backed consortium (includes Winning International, Shandong Weiqiao). GAC (Guinea Alumina Corporation) — Emirates Global Aluminium subsidiary. ASI (Aluminium Stewardship Initiative) — the industry sustainability standard for the aluminum value chain.

Ministère des Mines et de la Géologie — oversees minería policy and licensing. Code Minier (2011, revised 2013) — Guinea's minería code establishing ambiental obligations. EITI Guinea — Guinea has been EITI compliant, publishing reports on minería revenues.

Note: Guinea's democratic institutions are suspended under military rule. The Conseil National de la Transition (CNT) serves as a transitional legislature. International channels: ECOWAS (Guinea suspended), African Union, international minería company home-country oversight (UK, Canada, UAE, China). EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD) — will require EU companies to address human rights and ambiental impacts in their supply chains.

GQ.s: Small petrostate ($12.8B PIB) with 21.57% combined oil and gas rents. One of Africa's highest per-capita PIBs but extreme inequality. Authoritarian rule (Obiang family since 1979 — world's longest-serving head of state). Oil production declining from mature fields.

sectors.w: Equatorial Guinea's oil production has declined ~75% from peak. As fields deplete, the interés superpuesto between operators and government revenue se debilita. Without new discoveries, the economic case for diversification becomes unavoidable.
bp: Economic diversification as oil depletes. Cuando production revenue falls below government operating costs, el interés superpuesto en continued extraction from depleting fields breaks — alternative revenue sources become necessary.
Per-field emisiones disclosure through international operator reporting (ExxonMobil, Marathon Oil). Making ambiental costs visible makes W computable.
No se puede romper by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂.

Report corruption to US DOJ (several Obiang family members have faced kleptocracy investigations)

i: Note: Equatorial Guinea is one of the world's most autoritario states. No independent media, sociedad civil, or labor organizations exist. EG LNG (Equatorial Guinea LNG Holdings) — operates the LNG processing plant. International engagement: US Department of Justice Kleptocracy Asset Recovery Initiative has investigated Obiang family assets. Transparency International consistently ranks Equatorial Guinea among the most corrupt countries.
ExxonMobil — the largest oil operator in Equatorial Guinea. Marathon Oil — operates in offshore blocks. GEPetrol — the state national oil company.

Ministry of Mines and Hydrocarbons — oversees oil and gas operations. Note: Equatorial Guinea was suspended from EITI in 2010 for insufficient progress on transparencia. BEAC (central bank of CEMAC zone) provides limited macroeconomic oversight.

Note: Equatorial Guinea under President Obiang (in power since 1979) has no democratic institutions. The Cámara de Representantes del Pueblo and Senate are rubber-stamp bodies. International channels: CEMAC, African Union, UN Human Rights Council. Several countries (US, France, Switzerland) have pursued legal action against Obiang family members for corruption and money laundering.

GR.s: EU economía ($256.2B PIB) with dominant services sector (68% PIB, tourism-heavy). Low resource extraction. Post-debt-crisis recovery. Major shipping industry (Greek-owned fleet is the world's largest). Agricultura al 3.8% PIB (olives, wine).

sectors.w: Greece's Mediterranean diet is being displaced by UPF consumption, contributing to rising obesity (Greece has one of Europe's highest childhood obesity rates). Cuando diet-related healthcare costs are published per product category through EOPYY health insurance data, W se vuelve computable.
bp: Implementar front-of-pack health labelling to make welfare costs visible to consumers. Cuando UPF health costs are visible at point of sale, the interés superpuesto between processors and retailers in high-margin UPF se debilita.

EOPYY (National Organization for the Provision of Healthcare Services) publication of diet-attributable disease costs per food category. Greece's national health system generates data to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix. Reformarulation mandates can reduce dependencia del sistema.

i: EFET (ΕΦΕΤ — Ενιαίος Φορέας Ελέγχου Τροφίμων / Hellenic Food Authority) — Greece's food safety authority. Greece transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

SEVT (Σύνδεσμος Ελληνικών Βιομηχανιών Τροφίμων / Federation of Hellenic Food Industries) — the food industry trade association. Greece's traditional food sector (olive oil, dairy, honey) provides a competitive alternative to UPF.

Ministry of Health — oversees health policy. EOPYY (Εθνικός Οργανισμός Παροχής Υπηρεσιών Υγείας / National Organization for the Provision of Healthcare Services) — the national health insurer managing healthcare expenditure data.
Βουλή των Ελλήνων (Hellenic Parliament) — unicameral legislature with 300 members. Contact through hellenicparliament.gr. Greece is a parlamentaria democracia and miembro de la UE.

GT.s: Central American economía ($113.2B PIB) with agriculture at 9.8% PIB (coffee, sugar, palm oil). Mining at 1.12% PIB (gold, silver — Marlin mine controversy). High inequality and indigenous population (40%+). Remittances significant (~18% of PIB).

sectors.w: Guatemala's palm oil expansion into Petén and Alta Verapaz drives deforestación and displaces indigenous communities. Cuando per-farm deforestación data is linked to palm oil exports (EUDR compliance), W se vuelve computable.
bp: EU Deforestation Regulation compliance requiring farm-level traceability. Cuando palm oil exports must prove zero-deforestación origin, el interés superpuesto en clearing forest for plantations breaks.

Mandatory per-farm ambiental data (tree cover, water use, pesticide application). Guatemala's CONAP (National Council for Protected Areas) can provide monitoring data — linking to agricultural output makes W computable.
Parcialmente rompible. Palm oil monoculture reduces biodiversity. However, agroforestry integration and RSPO certification can reduce dependencia del sistema.

i: CONAP (Consejo Nacional de Áreas Protegidas) — manages Guatemala's protected areas. INAB (Instituto Nacional de Bosques) — the national forest institute. PDH (Procuraduría de los Derechos Humanos) — the national human rights ombudsman. Guatemala has significant challenges with ambiental defender safety.

REPSA (Reforestadora de Palma del Petén) — major palm oil producer. GREPALMA (Gremial de Palmicultores de Guatemala) — the palm oil producers' association. RSPO (Roundtable on Sustainable Aceite de Palma) — the international certification standard.
MARN (Ministerio de Ambiente y Recursos Naturales) — Guatemala's environment ministry. MAGA (Ministerio de Agricultura, Ganadería y Alimentación) — the agriculture ministry.

Congreso de la República de Guatemala — unicameral legislature with 160 members. Guatemala has ratified ILO Convention 169 on indigenous peoples' rights. The Inter-American Court of Human Rights has issued rulings on indigenous land rights in Guatemala.

GY.s: Fastest-growing economía globally ($24.7B PIB, up from $5.5B in 2019) due to massive offshore oil discoveries (Stabroek block). Rentas petroleras at 22.08%, minería at 9.38% (gold, bauxite). Small population (800K). At risk of 'resource curse' — extraordinary PST exposure from rapid petro-development.

sectors.w: Guyana's 2016 production sharing agreement with ExxonMobil has been widely criticized for giving the government only 2% royalty + 50% profit oil (after cost recovery). Cuando the full fiscal terms and ambiental costs are disclosed and understood, W se vuelve computable from production data.
bp: Renegotiate the Stabroek PSA fiscal terms to capture a fair share of petroleum wealth. Cuando Guyana's government share approaches international norms (60-80% government take), el interés superpuesto en an extractive-favorable contract se debilita.

Mandatory por campo emisiones disclosure and ambiental monitoring for all Stabroek block operations. ExxonMobil already reports global emisiones — extending to por campo Guyana data makes W computable.
No se puede romper by policy alone. Offshore oil production and combustion release CO₂.

Ministry of Natural Resources: Commission independent review of Stabroek PSA terms
Natural Resource Fund Board: Implementar transparent investment and withdrawal rules
Contacte a su Member of Parliament in the National Assembly para exigir independent review of Stabroek PSA fiscal terms
Support establishment of a properly resourced Petroleum Commission as independent regulator
Advocate for Natural Resource Fund transparencia aligned with Santiago Principles for sovereign wealth funds
