i: NEMC — autoridad ambiental. Mining Commission — minería sector regulator. LEAT — Tanzanian ambiental law NGO. HakiRasilimali (Publish What You Pay Tanzania) — revenue transparencia advocacy.

Twiga Minerals — 50/50 joint venture between Barrick Gold and Government of Tanzania (created after 2017 tax dispute). Geita Gold Mine — AngloGold Ashanti operation. STAMICO (State Mining Corporation) — government minería entity.
Mining Commission — established under Mining Act 2017. TEITI (Tanzania Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative) — EITI member since 2009.

Bunge la Tanzania (National Assembly) — unicameral, 393 members. Tanzania is a presidential republic with increasing political openness under current administration. Contact through bunge.go.tz. EAC (East African Community) member.

UA.s: War-affected economía ($190.7B PIB). Mining 5.32% PIB. Gasto militar 34.48% PIB (wartime). Agricultura al 7.1%. Coal 0.34%. candidato a la UE country (2022). Under Russian invasion since February 2022. Massive ambiental damage from war.

sectors.w: Ukraine's minería sector (iron ore, manganese, titanium) has suffered massive ambiental damage from the Russian invasion — flooded mines, destroyed infrastructure, contaminated land. Post-war reconstruction offers opportunity for transparent ambiental standards. Cuando reconstruction ambiental data is published, W se vuelve computable.
bp: EU accession conditionality requiring ambiental standards in post-war reconstruction. Cuando reconstruction adheres to EU ambiental acquis, el interés superpuesto en rapid but dirty rebuilding se debilita.
Environmental damage assessment and remediation tracking. Ukraine's Ministry of Environmental Protection is already documenting war damage with international support. Hace W computable.
Parcialmente rompible. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes, but post-war reconstruction can implement best-practice tailings management and water treatment from the start.
a: Informar a Derzhekoinspektsiia (State Environmental Inspectorate)
Documentar a través de OSCE ambiental monitoring
Report war-related ambiental damage through Ukraine's Ecodozor platform
Metinvest (Akhmetov): Implementar EU-aligned ambiental standards in reconstruction
ArcelorMittal Kryvyi Rih: Apply global ambiental standards
Reconstruction companies: Adoptar green building and ambiental remediation standards
Ministry of Environmental Protection: Hacer cumplir ambiental standards in reconstruction
State Subsoil Service: Reformar minería licensing with ambiental conditions
EU pre-accession assistance: Support ambiental governance capacity building
Contacte a su Narodnyi deputat in the Verkhovna Rada (450 members) para exigir ambiental standards in reconstruction legislation
Support Ukraine Recovery Conference ambiental commitments
Abogar a través de EU accession process for ambiental chapter prioritization
Presionar por international ambiental damage assessment and Russian liability for war-related ambiental destruction
i: Derzhekoinspektsiia — State Environmental Inspectorate. Ministry of Environmental Protection and Natural Resources — documents war-related ambiental damage. Ecodozor — citizen reporting platform.
Metinvest — Ukraine's largest steel and minería group (Rinat Akhmetov). ArcelorMittal Kryvyi Rih — major steel producer. Ferrexpo — iron ore pellet producer.
Ministry of Environmental Protection and Natural Resources. State Subsoil Service (Derzhgeonadra) — minería licensing. Ukraine's EU candidacy requires ambiental acquis alignment.

Verkhovna Rada — unicameral, 450 members. Contact through rada.gov.ua. candidato a la UE country since 2022. International channels: EU accession process, Ukraine Recovery Conference, International Criminal Court (ambiental damage documentation).

UG.s: East African economía ($53.9B PIB). Agricultura al 24.6% PIB. Rentas forestales 7.48% (one of the highest in the dataset). Oil production beginning (Tilenga and Kingfisher projects, EACOP pipeline). Authoritarian governance.

sectors.w: Uganda's new oil projects (TotalEnergies' Tilenga and CNOOC's Kingfisher, plus EACOP — East African Crude Oil Pipeline) face massive international scrutiny. Cuando project-level ambiental data is published, W se vuelve computable.
bp: International ESG pressure on project financiers. Cuando banks and insurers withdraw from EACOP financing, el interés superpuesto en unscrutinized pipeline construction se debilita. Several major banks have already declined.
Per-project ambiental impact data for Tilenga (in Murchison Falls National Park), Kingfisher, and EACOP. Hace W computable.
No se puede romper. Crude oil production and 1,443-km heated pipeline through sensitive ecosystems inherently create ambiental risk.
a: Informar a NEMA (National Environment Management Authority)
Documentar a través de international ambiental organizations (350.org, BankTrack)
Report through TotalEnergies compliance channels
TotalEnergies: Implementar comprehensive biodiversity offsets for Tilenga (inside Murchison Falls NP)
CNOOC Uganda: Adoptar international ambiental standards for Kingfisher
Banks: Apply Equator Principles to EACOP financing decisions
PAU (Petroleum Authority of Uganda): Hacer cumplir ambiental conditions in production licenses
NEMA: Fortalecer ambiental monitoring of oil projects
MEMD (Ministry of Energy and Mineral Desarrollarment): Ensure transparent revenue management
Support international ESG pressure on EACOP project financing
Abogar a través de European Parliament for TotalEnergies duty of vigilance enforcement (French Loi de Vigilance)
Presionar por EITI membership with ambiental disclosure
Support East African Court of Justice jurisdiction on transboundary ambiental issues
i: NEMA — autoridad ambiental. Note: Ugandan ambiental activists have faced government harassment. International channels are safer: 350.org has led the #StopEACOP campaign. BankTrack monitors EACOP financing.

TotalEnergies — operator of Tilenga project and EACOP. CNOOC Uganda — operates Kingfisher project. EACOP (East African Crude Oil Pipeline) — 1,443 km from Hoima to Tanga (Tanzania). UNOC (Uganda National Oil Company) — state oil company.
PAU — petroleum sector regulator. MEMD — energy and mineral development ministry. Uganda's Petroleum Revenue Management policy is under development.

Parliament of Uganda — unicameral, 529 members. Note: Uganda is effectively under one-party dominant governance (NRM). International channels: EAC (East African Community), African Court on Human and Peoples' Rights, European Parliament (regarding EU-headquartered companies like TotalEnergies).

UY.s: Small South American economía ($81B PIB). Agricultura al 6.4% PIB. Servicios al 65.3%. Rentas forestales 1.91%. Generates nearly 100% of electricity from renewables (wind, hydro, solar, biomass). Strong democracia — one of Latin America's most stable.

sectors.w: Uruguay's soy and eucalyptus monoculture expansion is degrading soils and displacing native grasslands (campos). Cuando soil health and biodiversity data is linked to agricultural production per department, W se vuelve computable.
bp: National soil conservation plan enforcement. Uruguay's Ley de Uso y Conservación de Suelos (Soil Use and Conservation Law) requires mandatory soil plans — strengthening enforcement breaks el interés superpuesto en soil-exhaustive monoculture.
Per-farm soil erosion and grassland conversion monitoring. MGAP already requires Plan de Uso y Manejo de Suelos for farms >50 ha. Publishing compliance data makes W computable.

Parcialmente rompible. Soy monoculture and eucalyptus plantations degrade native grassland ecosystems. Integrated crop-livestock systems and native grassland preservation reduce dependencia del sistema.
a: Informar a DINAMA (Dirección Nacional de Medio Ambiente)
Informar a RENARE (Dirección General de Recursos Naturales Renovables)
Documentar a través de Vida Silvestre Uruguay
Large agricultural companies: Fortalecer soil conservation plan compliance
UPM, Montes del Plata (forestry): Maintain native grassland corridors between eucalyptus plantations
Meat exporters: Adoptar pasture-based certification
MGAP (Ministerio de Ganadería, Agricultura y Pesca): Hacer cumplir mandatory soil plans
DINAMA: Fortalecer ambiental assessment for land-use change
INIA (Instituto Nacional de Investigación Agropecuaria): Publish soil health monitoring data
Contacte a su Diputado in the Cámara de Representantes (99 members) or Senador in the Cámara de Senadores (30 members) para exigir enforcement of the Soil Conservation Law
Support Mercosur ambiental cooperation frameworks
Advocate for native grassland (campos) conservation in international biodiversity frameworks
Presionar por soil carbon accounting in climate commitments
i: DINAMA — national environment directorate. RENARE — renewable natural resources directorate under MGAP. Vida Silvestre Uruguay — biodiversity conservation organization.
UPM (Finnish company) — major eucalyptus plantation and pulp mill operator. Montes del Plata (UPM/Arauco) — forestry. Large-scale soy farming often by Brazilian/Argentine operators.
MGAP — agriculture, livestock and fisheries ministry. INIA — national agricultural research institute.
Asamblea General — bicameral: Cámara de Representantes (99 members) and Cámara de Senadores (30 members). Contact through parlamento.gub.uy. Uruguay is one of Latin America's strongest democracies.

UZ.s: Central Asian economía ($115B PIB). Rentas de gas 11.04% PIB. Mining 8.5% (gold — Muruntau is world's largest open-pit gold mine, uranium). Agricultura al 18.3%. Manufactura al 20.2%. Post-Karimov liberalization (since 2016).

sectors.w: Uzbekistan's gas sector (Uzbekneftegaz) consumes most production domestically at heavily subsidized prices, encouraging waste. Cuando por campo production and domestic consumption data is published, W se vuelve computable.
bp: Gas price reform eliminating subsidies that encourage wasteful consumption. Cuando domestic gas prices reflect market value, el interés superpuesto en underpriced energy se debilita.
Per-field production, distribution, and consumption data. Uzbekistan has begun EITI implementation. Hace W computable.
Parcialmente rompible. Gas combustion emits CO₂. However, Uzbekistan's enormous solar potential (300+ sunny days/year) can displace gas in electricity generation.
a: Informar a State Committee for Ecology and Environmental Protection
Report through EITI Uzbekistan channels
Documentar a través de international partner organizations
Uzbekneftegaz: Publish por campo production and ambiental data
Navoi Mining (NMMC): Adoptar international ambiental standards for gold and uranium minería
Reduce methane leakage from gas infrastructure
Ministry of Energy: Implementar gas price reform roadmap
Goskomekologiya: Fortalecer ambiental enforcement at minería and gas facilities
EITI Uzbekistan: Expand to ambiental data disclosure
Engage through Oliy Majlis (parliament) — bicameral: Legislative Chamber (150 members) and Senate (100 members)
Support EITI enhanced reporting with ambiental data
Abogar a través de EBRD and ADB for gas price reform conditionality
Presionar por Aral Sea ambiental restoration international cooperation
i: State Committee for Ecology and Environmental Protection (Goskomekologiya) — ambiental regulator. EITI Uzbekistan — Uzbekistan became an EITI member in 2021.
Uzbekneftegaz — state gas company. NMMC (Navoi Mining and Metallurgical Company) — operates Muruntau gold mine (world's largest open-pit) and uranium mines. Almalyk MMC — copper and gold minería.
Ministry of Energy — energy policy. Goskomekologiya — ambiental regulation. Uzbekistan has been implementing significant economic reforms since 2016.

Oliy Majlis — bicameral: Legislative Chamber (Qonunchilik palatasi, 150 members) and Senate, 100 members. Note: limited political pluralism. International channels: EITI, EBRD, ADB, SCO. Aral Sea ambiental disaster requires international cooperation.

VE.s: Oil-dependent economía in crisis ($119.8B PIB — severely contracted from peak). World's largest proven oil reserves. Economic data largely unavailable (World Bank reports zeros for most indicators). Hyperinflation, mass emigration. Authoritarian governance.

sectors.w: Venezuela's oil industry has collapsed from ~3.3M bpd (1990s) to ~700K bpd due to mismanagement, underinvestment, and sanciones. The interés superpuesto is already weakened by operational failure.
bp: Production collapse has already weakened the interés superpuesto — PDVSA lacks the capital and technical capacity to maintain production. Sanctions further constrain revenue.
Oil revenue transparencia is impossible under current governance opacity. However, international sanciones monitoring (OFAC designations) provides partial W computability.
No se puede romper. Oil extraction and refining emit CO₂. Venezuela's heavy crude (Orinoco Belt) is particularly emisiones-intensive to process.
a: Report through international channels (Transparencia Venezuela, PROVEA)
Documentar a través de exile media and research organizations
Provide data to international sanciones enforcement authorities
International oil companies with remaining interests (Chevron, Repsol, ENI): Apply ambiental standards to limited operations
Chevron: Publish per-facility ambiental data for Venezuela operations
Service companies: Maintain ambiental standards in contracted work
OFAC: Maintain sanciones conditionality linked to governance reform
EU: Coordinate sanciones policy
OPEC: Address Venezuela's production reporting transparencia
Support international sanciones conditionality requiring democratic governance and ambiental standards
Abogar a través de Lima Group/International Contact Group for governance reform
Presionar por PDVSA operational and ambiental transparencia as condition of sanciones relief
Support post-transition EITI membership planning
i: Note: No independent domestic institutions exist. Transparencia Venezuela — anti-corruption NGO. PROVEA — Venezuelan human rights organization. Operating environment is extremely hostile to sociedad civil.
PDVSA (Petróleos de Venezuela, S.A.) — state oil company, severely degraded operationally. Chevron — received limited US OFAC license to operate. Repsol, ENI — maintain limited presence.

No independent domestic regulation exists. OFAC (Office of Foreign Assets Control, US Treasury) — administers Venezuela sanciones. EU — maintains sanciones. PDVSA is both regulator and operator — no separation exists.

Asamblea Nacional — 277 members. Note: Venezuela's institutions lack independence under current governance. International channels: OAS (Venezuela withdrawn/suspended), International Contact Group, ICC preliminary examination, Lima Group (reduced activity).

VN.s: Fast-growing economía ($476.4B PIB). Manufactura al 24.4% PIB. Agricultura al 11.9%. Rentas de carbón 0.38%. Oil 0.67%. Rentas forestales 1.07%. Major electronics and textile manufacturing hub. One-party state (Communist Party).

sectors.w: Viet Nam is rapidly expanding coal power capacity despite excellent renewable potential (solar, wind, offshore wind). JETP (Just Energy Transition Partnership) funding ($15.5B pledged) creates leverage to shift the interés superpuesto away from coal.
bp: JETP implementation — $15.5B pledged by international partners for coal-to-renewables transition. Cuando JETP funding makes renewables cheaper than new coal, el interés superpuesto en coal expansion breaks.
Per-plant emisiones and health cost data. Viet Nam's coal plants are concentrated in the north (Quảng Ninh) and south (Bình Thuận). Hace W computable.
No se puede romper. Coal combustion releases SO₂, PM2.5, and CO₂.
a: Informar a MONRE (Bộ Tài nguyên và Môi trường — Ministry of Natural Resources and Environment)
Documentar a través de VUSTA (Viet Nam Union of Science and Technology Associations)
Report through international development partner channels (World Bank, ADB)
EVN (Electricity of Viet Nam): Accelerate Power Desarrollarment Plan VIII renewable targets
PVN (PetroVietnam): Invertir en offshore wind
International manufacturers (Samsung, Intel): Demand renewable electricity procurement options
MOIT (Ministry of Industry and Trade): Implementar PDP VIII renewable targets
MONRE: Fortalecer emission standards for coal plants

Support JETP implementation monitoring through international partner coordination
Abogar a través de ASEAN for regional energy transition cooperation
Presionar por JETP transparencia and accountability mechanisms
Support World Bank and ADB energía renovable financing for Viet Nam
i: MONRE — environment and natural resources ministry. VUSTA — umbrella organization for Vietnamese sociedad civil (within party framework). Note: independent sociedad civil is not permitted in Viet Nam's one-party system.

EVN (Tập đoàn Điện lực Việt Nam) — state electricity utility. PVN (PetroVietnam) — state oil and gas company. TKV (Vinacomin — Tập đoàn Công nghiệp Than - Khoáng sản Việt Nam) — state coal minería company.

MOIT — energy and industrial policy. PDP VIII (Power Desarrollarment Plan VIII, approved 2023) — sets energía renovable targets. JETP — Just Energy Transition Partnership ($15.5B pledged by IPG partners including US, EU, UK, Japan).

Quốc hội (National Assembly) — unicameral, 500 members. Viet Nam is a estado de partido único (Communist Party). JETP International Partners Group provides the primary leverage mechanism. International channels: ASEAN, World Bank, ADB, JETP IPG.

XK.s: Small Western Balkan economía ($11.2B PIB). Manufactura al 12.6%. Rentas de carbón 0.38%. Mining 0.55%. Agricultura al 7.3%. Youngest population in Europe. candidato a la UE status pending. Two aging lignite plants (Kosovo A and B) supply ~95% of electricity.

sectors.w: Kosovo's near-total dependence on two aging lignite plants (Kosovo A — one of Europe's worst polluters — and Kosovo B) faces EU accession conditionality and enormous health costs.
bp: EU accession pathway requiring IED/LCPD compliance. Kosovo A is scheduled for decommissioning. Cuando ambiental compliance costs are applied, new renewables are cheaper than coal retrofit.
Per-plant emisiones and health cost data. Kosovo A and B cause severe air pollution in Pristina — among Europe's worst air quality. Hace W computable.
No se puede romper. Lignite combustion releases SO₂, PM2.5, mercury, and CO₂.
a: Informar a AMMK (Agjencia për Mbrojtjen e Mjedisit të Kosovës — Kosovo Environmental Protection Agency)
Documentar a través de INDEP or GAP Institute (Kosovo think tanks)
Report through KEK compliance channels
KEK (Korporata Energjetike e Kosovës): Plan Kosovo A decommissioning and Kosovo B modernization or replacement
Invertir en solar and wind capacity
Implementar just transition plans for Obiliq/Kastriot minería communities
ERO (Energy Regulatory Office): Reformar tariffs to incentivize renewable investment
MMPH (Ministry of Environment): Hacer cumplir emission standards
Energy Community Secretariat: Hold Kosovo accountable for compliance
Contacte a su Deputet in the Kuvendi i Kosovës (120 members) para exigir Kosovo A decommissioning timeline and energía renovable investment
Support EU integration benchmarks on ambiental standards
Abogar a través de Energy Community for just transition support
Presionar por World Bank and EBRD financing of energía renovable replacement
i: AMMK — ambiental protection agency. INDEP (Institute for Desarrollarment Policy) — research and advocacy. GAP Institute — Kosovo public policy think tank.
KEK — Kosovo Energy Corporation, estatal, operates Kosovo A (5 units, 800 MW, built 1962-1975) and Kosovo B (2 units, 678 MW, built 1983-1984). KOSTT — transmission system operator.
ERO — regulador energético. MMPH (Ministria e Mjedisit, Planifikimit Hapësinor dhe Infrastrukturës) — environment ministry. Kosovo is an Energy Community Treaty member.

Kuvendi i Kosovës (Assembly of Kosovo) — unicameral, 120 members. Contact through kuvendikosoves.org. Kosovo declared independence in 2008 (recognized by ~100 UN members). Energy Community Treaty member.

ZA.s: Largest sub-Saharan economía ($401.1B PIB). Rentas de carbón 2.44% PIB. Mining 3.83% (gold, platinum group metals, chrome, manganese). Manufactura al 12.8%. Servicios al 63%. Constitutional democracia. Severe electricity crisis (load shedding).

sectors.w: South Africa generates ~80% of electricity from coal (Eskom's aging fleet). JETP funding ($8.5B pledged) and Eskom's operational crisis (load shedding) create dual pressure. Cuando coal plant health costs are internalized, the interés superpuesto breaks.
bp: JETP implementation and Eskom restructuring. $8.5B pledged for coal-to-renewables transition. Cuando renewable IPPs are cheaper and more reliable than aging coal plants, el interés superpuesto en coal dependence breaks.

Per-plant emisiones, health costs, and reliability data. Eskom's coal fleet is extensively documented — multiple plants fail to meet South Africa's own emission standards (granted repeated postponements). Hace W computable.
No se puede romper. Coal combustion releases SO₂, PM2.5, mercury, and CO₂. South Africa's coal is high-ash content, exacerbating particulate emisiones.
a: Informar a DFFE (Department of Forestry, Pesca and the Environment)
Report through groundWork or Centre for Environmental Rights
Documentar a través de Public Protector complaints
Eskom: Implementar coal plant decommissioning schedule aligned with IRP 2019
Mining companies (Exxaro, Thungela, Seriti): Desarrollar coal transition plans
Renewable IPPs: Scale deployment to replace coal capacity
NERSA (National Energy Regulator): Reformar tariffs to reflect coal externalities
DFFE: Hacer cumplir Minimum Emission Standards without further postponements
DMRE (Department of Mineral Resources and Energy): Accelerate IRP 2019 renewable targets
Contacte a su Member of Parliament in the National Assembly (400 members) or NCOP (National Council of Provinces, 90 members) para exigir Eskom emission standard compliance
Support JETP implementation and accountability mechanisms
Abogar a través de BRICS for just energy transition principles
Presionar por climate finance delivery through Green Climate Fund
i: DFFE — the environment department. groundWork — South African ambiental justice organization. Centre for Environmental Rights — public interest ambiental law organization. Life After Coal campaign — coalition challenging Eskom's coal dependence.

Eskom — state electricity utility, operates 15 coal-fired power stations. Exxaro Resources — coal minería. Thungela Resources — thermal coal (spun off from Anglo American). Seriti Resources — coal minería. REIPPPP (Renewable Energy Independent Power Producer Procurement Programme) — auction program.

NERSA — regulador energético. DMRE — mineral resources and energy department. IRP 2019 (Integrated Resource Plan) — South Africa's electricity plan. JETP Investment Plan — guides $8.5B transition funding.

Parliament of South Africa — bicameral: National Assembly (400 members) and National Council of Provinces (90 members). Contact through parliament.gov.za. South Africa is a constitutional democracia, G20 member, BRICS member. JETP International Partners Group: France, Germany, UK, US, EU.

ZM.s: Southern African economía ($25.3B PIB). Mining 28.25% PIB — one of the highest minería dependencies globally (copper). Rentas forestales 6.78%. Servicios al 57.2%. Africa's second-largest copper producer. Democratic governance with peaceful power transitions.

sectors.w: Zambia's copper minería generates acid mine drainage and SO₂ from smelting (Mopani, KCM — historically among Africa's worst polluters). Cuando por mina ambiental data is published, W se vuelve computable.
bp: Mining royalty reform with ambiental earmarking. Cuando minería revenue is partly allocated to ambiental remediation, el interés superpuesto en externalizing pollution costs se debilita.
Per-mine ambiental monitoring data through ZEITI and ZEMA. Hace W computable.
Parcialmente rompible. Copper smelting emisiones can be reduced with modern sulfuric acid capture technology. Mopani smelter upgrade demonstrates this.
a: Informar a ZEMA (Zambia Environmental Management Agency)
Documentar a través de community organizations in Copperbelt Province
Report through minería company compliance channels
First Quantum Minerals (Kansanshi): Implementar international ambiental standards
Barrick Gold (Lumwana): Adoptar enhanced tailings management
ZCCM-IH (state minería holding): Require ambiental compliance from partners
ZEMA: Hacer cumplir emission standards for copper smelters
Ministry of Mines: Fortalecer ambiental conditions in minería licenses
ZEITI: Expand to include ambiental data
Contacte a su Member of Parliament in the National Assembly (156 members) para exigir ambiental enforcement on the Copperbelt
Support EITI enhanced reporting with ambiental data
Abogar a través de SADC for regional minería ambiental standards
Presionar por responsible mineral supply chain standards (copper is critical for energy transition)
i: ZEMA — ambiental management agency. Copperbelt communities have suffered decades of SO₂ and heavy metal pollution — documented by multiple studies and legal cases (e.g., Vedanta/KCM litigation in UK courts).

First Quantum Minerals — operates Kansanshi (one of Africa's largest copper mines) and Sentinel mines. Barrick Gold — operates Lumwana copper mine. Mopani Copper Mines — majority owned by ZCCM-IH after Glencore divestment. KCM (Konkola Copper Mines) — under provisional liquidation.
Ministry of Mines and Minerals Desarrollarment. ZEITI (Zambia Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative) — EITI member. Mining Cadastre Department — licensing authority.

National Assembly of Zambia — unicameral, 156 elected members + 8 nominated. Contact through parliament.gov.zm. Zambia is a presidential republic with competitive elections (peaceful transitions in 1991, 2011, 2021). SADC (Southern African Desarrollarment Community) member.

ZW.s: Southern African economía ($41.5B PIB). Mining 4.24% PIB (platinum group metals, gold, lithium, diamonds). Agricultura al 8.7%. Manufactura al 15.6%. Rentas forestales 1.82%. Governance concerns. Major lithium reserves (Bikita, Arcadia).

sectors.w: Zimbabwe's minería sector is expanding rapidly (lithium — critical for EV batteries — and PGMs). Cuando por mina ambiental data is published alongside revenue, W se vuelve computable.
bp: International supply chain due diligence for critical minerals (lithium for EU Battery Regulation compliance). Cuando EU buyers require ambiental verification, el interés superpuesto en unregulated extraction se debilita.
Per-mine ambiental monitoring through ZEITI (Zimbabwe applied for EITI candidacy). Hace W computable.
Parcialmente rompible. Lithium minería (hard-rock) generates waste rock and uses water. Best-practice processing and water recycling reduce dependencia del sistema.
a: Informar a EMA (Environmental Management Agency)
Documentar a través de ZELA (Zimbabwe Environmental Law Association)
Report through international minería company compliance channels

Prospect Lithium (Bikita Minerals): Adoptar responsible lithium minería practices
Zim Integrated (Arcadia): Apply EU Battery Regulation-aligned ambiental standards
Ministry of Mines: Hacer cumplir ambiental conditions in minería licenses
EMA: Fortalecer ambiental impact assessment for lithium projects
ZMDC (Zimbabwe Mining Desarrollarment Corporation): Require ambiental compliance from joint venture partners
Engage through available parlamentaria channels — Parliament of Zimbabwe: National Assembly (270 members) and Senate (80 members); note limited political pluralism in practice
Support EU Battery Regulation supply chain due diligence requirements for Zimbabwean lithium
Abogar a través de SADC Mining Protocol for regional ambiental standards
Presionar por EITI membership to improve minería transparencia
i: EMA — ambiental management agency. ZELA — ambiental law organization. Note: sociedad civil space is constrained in Zimbabwe.

Zimplats — Zimbabwe's largest PGM producer (Impala Platinum subsidiary). Bikita Minerals — lithium minería (Sinomine Resources acquired). Arcadia Lithium — major lithium deposit (Zhejiang Huayou Cobalt acquired). Caledonia Mining — gold.
Ministry of Mines and Mining Desarrollarment. EMA — ambiental regulation. ZMDC — state minería company. Zimbabwe's Mines and Minerals Act governs the sector.

Parliament of Zimbabwe — bicameral: National Assembly (270 members) and Senate (80 members). Note: ZANU-PF dominance limits effective legislative oversight. International channels: SADC, AU, EITI (Zimbabwe has expressed interest in joining).

CN.s: World's second-largest economía ($18,743.8B PIB). Manufactura al 24.9% PIB — global factory floor. Rentas de carbón 0.61%. Industria al 36.5%. Servicios al 56.7%. One-party state (CPC). World's largest CO₂ emitter. Rapid renewable deployment alongside continued coal expansion.

sectors.w: China operates ~1,100 GW of coal capacity — more than the rest of the world combined. However, China also deploys more energía renovable annually than any other country. Cuando renewable deployment makes new coal uneconomic, el interés superpuesto en coal expansion breaks.
bp: Renewable energy cost competitiveness — China's solar and wind are already cheaper than new coal in most provinces. Cuando existing coal plants become more expensive to run than new renewables, the interés superpuesto breaks. This is already happening in eastern provinces.
Per-plant emisiones data. China's national ETS (launched 2021) covers ~4.5 billion tonnes CO₂. Expanding coverage from electricity to industry sectors makes W computable.
No se puede romper. Coal combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, PM2.5, and mercury by chemistry.
a: Informar a MEE (Ministry of Ecology and Environment, 生态环境部)
Report through provincial ambiental protection bureaus
Documentar a través de 12369 ambiental complaint hotline
State energy companies (CHN Energy, Huaneng, Datang, Huadian, SPIC): Accelerate coal-to-renewables transition
Reduce new coal plant approvals
Invertir en grid-scale storage to enable renewable integration
NEA (National Energy Administration, 国家能源局): Limit new coal approvals to replace-and-retire
MEE: Accelerate national ETS coverage expansion to cement, steel, aluminum
NDRC: Align electricity market reform with carbon reduction targets
Support US-China climate cooperation mechanisms (Sunnylands process)
Abogar a través de COP process for China's coal phase-down commitments
Presionar por national ETS carbon price to rise above marginal abatement cost
Support technology cooperation on CCS and grid-scale storage
i: MEE (Ministry of Ecology and Environment) — central autoridad ambiental. 12369 hotline — ambiental complaint system receiving millions of reports annually. Provincial Environmental Protection Bureaus — local enforcement. IPE (Institute of Public & Environmental Affairs, 公众环境研究中心) — Ma Jun's NGO providing corporate ambiental data.

China's 'Big Five' power generators: CHN Energy (国家能源集团), China Huaneng (华能), China Datang (大唐), China Huadian (华电), SPIC (国家电投). All estatal, collectively operating most coal capacity. Also rapidly scaling renewables.

NEA (National Energy Administration) — energy planning and regulation. NDRC (National Desarrollarment and Reformar Commission, 国家发展和改革委员会) — economic planning, sets electricity prices. National ETS — world's largest carbon market by coverage (power sector).

National People's Congress (全国人民代表大会) — ~3,000 members. China is a estado de partido único — the NPC does not function as an independent legislature. International channels: US-China climate bilateral, COP/UNFCCC, G20. China's dual carbon targets: peak CO₂ before 2030, carbon neutrality by 2060.

KR.s: Advanced economía ($1,875.4B PIB). Manufactura al 26.6% PIB (semiconductors, automobiles, shipbuilding, petrochemicals). Servicios al 57.5%. Heavy energy import dependence. Fourth-largest coal importer globally. G20 member. Vibrant democracia.

sectors.w: South Korea operates ~37 GW of coal capacity and is the world's 4th-largest coal importer. The 10th Basic Plan for Electricity Supply and Demand targets coal phase-down. Cuando carbon pricing (K-ETS) makes coal uncompetitive versus LNG and renewables, the interés superpuesto breaks.
bp: K-ETS (Korea Emissions Trading Scheme) carbon price escalation. Cuando carbon costs make coal-fired generation more expensive than LNG and renewables, el interés superpuesto en coal dependence breaks.
Per-plant emisiones and health cost data. Korea Environment Institute and Korean medical associations have documented coal plant health impacts. Hace W computable.
No se puede romper. Coal combustion releases SO₂, PM2.5, and CO₂.
a: Informar a Ministry of Environment (환경부)
Documentar a través de KFEM (Korean Federation for Environmental Movements, 환경운동연합)
Report through KEPCO compliance channels

i: Ministry of Environment (환경부) — autoridad ambiental. KFEM (환경운동연합) — South Korea's largest ambiental NGO. Solutions for Our Climate (기후솔루션) — climate policy advocacy organization.
KEPCO — state electricity company (subsidiary gencos operate coal plants). POSCO — steelmaker, major coal consumer. Samsung, SK Group, Hyundai — major conglomerates with growing RE100 commitments.
MOTIE — energy and industry ministry. K-ETS — Korea Emissions Trading Scheme (launched 2015, covers ~70% of national emisiones). KPX (한국전력거래소) — electricity market operator.

국회 (National Assembly) — unicameral, 300 members. Contact through assembly.go.kr. South Korea is a presidential republic with competitive elections. G20 member, OECD member, Powering Past Coal Alliance member (joined 2021).
